sanskrit: मम योनिर्महद्ब्रह्म तस्मिन् गर्भं दधाम्यहम् । संभवः सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत ॥
hindiTranslation: हे भरतवंशोद्भव अर्जुन  मेरी मूल प्रकृति तो उत्पत्तिस्थान है और मैं उसमें जीवरूप गर्भका स्थापन करता हूँ ।  उससे सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंकी उत्पत्ति होती है ॥
englishTranslation: O Bharata, my primal nature is the womb; I place the seed in it. From that, all beings are born.
essence: All birth comes from a source beyond personal control.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: All living forms arise from the union of primal nature and the seed placed within it. Krishna is explaining how birth itself unfolds.
simpleInsight: The world is not random. Life arises through a deeper order already at work.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, with Arjuna still caught between collapse and action, Krishna shifts from philosophy to cosmic explanation. After speaking of the supreme reality, he explains how all beings come into birth: primal nature is the source, and the seed is placed within it.
modernRelevance: A child’s first heartbeat appears on a screen, and no one in the room can explain life in a single sentence. The verse reminds you that existence comes through forces larger than personal control.
translationLiteral: मम (my) / योनिः (womb, source) / महत्-ब्रह्म (great nature) / तस्मिन् (in that) / गर्भम् (seed, embryo) / दधामि (I place) / अहम् (I) / संभवः (coming into being, birth) / सर्व-भूतानाम् (of all beings) / ततः (from that) / भवति (comes to be) / भारत (O Bharata)
insight: It shifts attention from isolated individuality to the hidden process that generates every form.
